fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan OR FD-258 hard card via mail

seo.title: Idaho CNA Certification Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your Certification (2026)
seo.description: Already certified as a CNA? Here's whether your certification transfers to Idaho - endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: CNA license reciprocity idaho, how to transfer CNA license to idaho, idaho CNA endorsement application

board.name: Idaho Board of Nursing
board.mailing_address:
Idaho Board of Nursing
P.O. Box 83720
Boise, ID 83720-0061

license_types.title: Certified Nursing Assistant (CNA)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a nursing assistant in Idaho.

steps.title: Create Portal Account
steps.description: Go to the Idaho Board of Nursing Applicant Portal (ibn.idaho.gov/applicant-portal/) and create a new user account. You will need to provide personal details and set up your login credentials.

steps.title: Initiate Application
steps.description: Log in to your newly created Applicant Portal account. From your dashboard, click 'Begin a New Application' and select 'Nurse Aide' as the application type. Choose either 'Certification by Examination' or 'Certification by Endorsement' based on your eligibility.

steps.title: Complete Application Form
steps.description: Proceed to fill out all required sections of the online application form. This includes providing your personal information, detailed education or training history, and answering background questions.

steps.title: Upload Supporting Documents
steps.description: Electronically upload all necessary supporting documentation directly through the Applicant Portal. This may include proof of completion from an approved training program, official exam results, or verification of an out-of-state license for endorsement.

steps.title: Pay Application Fee
steps.description: Submit the required non-refundable application fee of $40 for either certification by examination or endorsement. Payment is made securely via credit or debit card within the Applicant Portal.

steps.title: Submit and Monitor Application
steps.description: Review your entire application and all uploaded documents for accuracy and completeness. Once satisfied, electronically submit your application. You can monitor the status and any communications regarding your application directly through your Idaho Board of Nursing Applicant Portal dashboard.

documents.detail: Certificate of completion from your approved CNA training program.
Official verification of passing your state's nurse aide competency evaluation.

provisional_text: Some states allow CNAs to work under supervision for a limited period while their endorsement application is being processed.
reciprocity_text: CNAs certified in another state can often apply via endorsement (reciprocity) by providing registry verification from their home state.

faqs.question: How much does Idaho CNA endorsement cost?
faqs.answer: The endorsement application fee is $25. Additional costs may include background check fees.

faqs.question: Is a background check required?
faqs.answer: Yes, a fingerprint-based background check is required in Idaho.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my CNA certification to Idaho?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Idaho while waiting?
faqs.answer: Check with the state board about temporary practice permits while your endorsement is being processed.

reciprocity.notes: CNAs certified in another state can often apply via endorsement (reciprocity) by providing registry verification from their home state.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become a CNA in ID.

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: Transfer an active out-of-state CNA to Idaho.

hero.title: Become a CNA in Idaho
hero.dek: To apply for a Certified Nursing Assistant license in Idaho, you must create an account and submit your application through the Idaho Board of Nursing Applicant Portal.

seo.title: How to Become a CNA in Idaho
seo.description: Step-by-step guide to Idaho CNA certification.

official_links.portal_name: Idaho Board of Nursing Applicant Portal
official_links.registration_steps: Go to the Idaho Board of Nursing Applicant Portal and click on 'New users: Create Account' to register your profile. After creating your account, log in to your dashboard to begin a new application for a Nurse Aide certification.
